revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Amenohoakari]][[fr:Ame-no-hoakari]][[it:Amenohoakari]][[ja:天火明命]][[ko:아메노호아카리]][[mni:ꯑꯃꯦꯅꯣꯍꯣꯑꯀꯔꯤ]][[simple:Ame-no-hoakari]][[zh:天火明命]]
{{Short description|Japanese deity|qq=}} {{Page numbers needed|date=August 2021|qq=}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|qid=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} | mother = [[Takuhadachiji-hime]] | children = {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} | gender = {{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}} | parents ={{ill|Oshihomimi|qid=Q462555|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}; {{ill|Takuhata Chijihime no Mikoto|qid=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}}| offspring = {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|zh|天香山命}} | relatives = {{ill|Q20041346|qid=Q20041346|1=Q20041346}} | equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|qid=Q10940674|1=Ame no Wakahiko}}; {{ill|Honoakari|qid=Q60673696|1=Honoakari}}; {{ill|Honosusori|qid=Q10731395|1=Hosuseri}}; {{ill|Nigihayahi|qid=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}} }} {{Nihongo|'''Amenohoakari'''|天火明|qq=}} is a god of {{ill|Solar deity|en|Solar deity|qid=Q511056|lt=sun|1=Solar deity}} and [[:en:God of agriculture|agriculture]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. His descendants are called ''[[Tenson-zoku]]''.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|date=1983|publisher=OnkogaCategoryai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|qq=}}</ref> == Name == Names for Amenohoakari are listed below. * Amaterukuniteruhikoamenohoakarikushitamanigihayahi-no-mikoto (天照国照彦天火明櫛玉饒速日尊) in ''[[Kujiki]]'' * Amaterukuniteruhikoamenohoakari-no-mikoto (天照國照彦天火明尊) * Nigihayahi-no-mikoto (饒速日命) * Amaterukuniteruhikohoakari-no-mikoto (天照国照彦火明命) in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' * Amenohoakari-no-mikoto (天火明命) in ''[[Kojiki]]'' * Hoakari-no-mikoto (火明命) in ''Nihon Shoki'' * Ikishiniho-no-mikoto (膽杵磯丹杵穂命) * Amaterumitama-no-kami (天照御魂神) in ''Jinjyashiryō'' == Genealogy == According to ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Amenohoakari was born to {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|qid=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} and [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi's]] daughter, [[:en:Yorozuhatahime|Yorozuhatahime]]. [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] is his younger brother, but in another book of ''Nihon Shoki'', Ninigi is his father. Otherwise, in book of ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Nigihayahi is offspring of [[Amatsukami]]. In ''[[Kujiki]]'', Amenohoakari is considered the same ''kami'' as {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, the ancestral god of the Hozumi and [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] clans, but this contradicts their generational relationships and areas of activity.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|year=2007|location=Japan|qq=}}</ref> In ''Harima-no-kuni Fudoki'', Amenohoakari is the child of [[Ōkuninushi]] and Dotsuhime (弩都比売).<ref>{{Cite book|last=Okimori|first=Takuya|title=Harima-no-kuni Fudoki|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2005|isbn=4634593912|location=Japan|qq=}}</ref> == Descendants == In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'', the descendants of [[Amatsuhikone]], [[Ame no Hohi]], and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|qq=}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as ''Tenson-zoku''. The ''Tenson-zoku'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en|Heaven|qid=Q4489450|lt=Heaven|1=Heaven}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|qid=Q514506|lt=Owari|1=Owari Province}} and {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|qid=Q954511|lt=Tanba|1=Tanba Province}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of [[Owari clan|Owari]], {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, [[Amabe clan|Amabe]], and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|1=Tanba clan|2=ja|3=丹波氏|4=ko|5=단바씨|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|qq=}} clans.<ref name=":0" /> However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||qq=}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of Amenohoakari, is a forged document,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Kokuho「Amabe-shi Keizu」he no gimon, Kokigi no Heya (国宝「海部氏系図」への疑問 古樹紀之房間)|year=2006|location=Japan|qq=}}</ref> and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|en|List of water deities|qid=Q13416336|lt=sea deity|1=List of water deities}} {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|qid=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, [[Takakuraji]], as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|year=2006|location=Japan|qq=}}</ref> The [[Mononobe clan]] is a clan whose ancestor was Nigihayahi. The Hozumi clan and the Kumano no Kunizukuri Wada clan are said to have the same ancestor. The Yuge clan is closely related to the Mononobe clan.<ref name=":03">{{Cite web |last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及 |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus,デジタル大辞泉,世界大百科事典 |title=饒速日命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%A5%92%E9%80%9F%E6%97%A5%E5%91%BD-591173 |access-date=2023-01-06 |website=コトバンク |language=ja|qq=}}</ref> A genealogical tree handed down to the Kaifu clan, the family of the Kagome Shrine, has been designated a national treasure.{{Efn|The authenticity of the contents is questionable|qq=}}{{Efn|The Kaifu clan, like the Owari clan, are descendants of Amanohiyakumei-no-mikoto and not of Nineiyakumei-no-mikoto|qq=}} == Name meaning == As Amenohoakari's name suggests, he is the deification of sunlight and heat. In ''{{ill|Kojiki-den|en|Kojiki-den|qid=Q11410835|lt=Kojiki-den|1=Kojiki-den}}'', Hoakari is written "穂赤熟", which means that the ears of rice ripen and become red. Like the other gods connected to the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}}, Amenohoakari's name is also related to rice and is worshiped as the sun god and the god of agriculture.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Motoori|first=Norinaga|title=Kojiki-den|publisher=Nabu Press|year=2011|isbn=978-1178814958|qq=}}</ref> ==Tomb== [[File:饒速日尊の墳墓.jpg|thumb|Tomb of Nigihayahi]] The tomb of Nigihayahi-no-mikoto is located on Mount Shiraniwa in Shiraniwadai, Ikoma City, Nara Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|title=饒速日命墳墓 · 日本、〒630-0136 奈良県生駒市白庭台５丁目９−１|url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E3%80%92630-0136+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E7%94%9F%E9%A7%92%E5%B8%82%E7%99%BD%E5%BA%AD%E5%8F%B0%EF%BC%95%E4%B8%81%E7%9B%AE%EF%BC%99%E2%88%92%EF%BC%91+%E9%A5%92%E9%80%9F%E6%97%A5%E5%91%BD%E5%A2%B3%E5%A2%93/@34.7186725,135.7121715,16z/data=!4m2!3m1!1s0x6001225829781f87:0x8151fcb18e0163e9?authuser=0&hl=ja|access-date=2022-01-13|website=饒速日命墳墓 · 日本、〒630-0136 奈良県生駒市白庭台５丁目９−１|language=ja-US|qq=}}</ref> == Shrines to worship == * Iwafune Shrine - The shrine's deity is a huge rock called "Ame-no-Iwafune" (Heavenly Rock). * Amateru Tamamikoto Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|en|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|qid=Q3355306|lt=Fukuchiyama City|1=Fukuchiyama, Kyoto}}, Imaan) * Ishikiri Kenjya Shrine ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|qid=Q243863|lt=Higashi Osaka City|1=Higashiōsaka}} Higashi Ishikiri-cho) * {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro Ōji|lt=Fujishiro-ōji|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro-ōji|qq=}} ({{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}} {{ill|Kainan, Wakayama|en|Kainan, Wakayama|qid=Q841309|lt=Kainan City|1=Kainan, Wakayama}}) * [[Hirose Taisha|Hirose Taisha]] ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}[[Kitakatsuragi District, Nara|Kitakatsuragi County]]{{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|qid=Q1203880|lt=Kawai Town|1=Kawai, Nara}}) * Yada-za Kushiyamahiko Shrine (Yada-cho, {{ill|Yamatokōriyama|en|Yamatokōriyama|qid=Q118213|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, Nara Prefecture) * {{ill|Hikō Shrine|lt=Hikō Shrine|lt=Hiko Shrine|ja|飛行神社|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Shrine|qq=}} - Founded in the Taisho era as a god of airplanes. Reihayakuninushi-no-mikoto is regarded as the ancestor god of aviation based on the old saying, "Riding on a celestial ship, flying through the sky,"<ref>"Nihon Shoki Vol. 3 Jimmu Emperor Ki"</ref> and is also worshipped as the god of the sky.<ref>See {{ill|Hikō Shrine|lt=Hikō Shrine|lt=Hiko Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Shrine|qq=}}</ref> * {{ill|Isekisan Shrine|ja|井関三神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isekisan Shrine|qq=}} - Amateru Shrine was founded in {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} 2 (BC {{ill|AD 96|en|AD 96|qid=Q31365|lt=96|1=AD 96}}), enshrining a huge rocky platform as the sacred body of Amateru Kuniteruhiko Himyo Kushitama Reihatsu-no-mikoto. * Kunitsuhikonomikoto Shrine ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}} {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City|1=Matsuyama}}, Hachitanchi) * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|en|Mononobe Shrine|qid=Q11570699|lt=Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe Shrine (Oda)}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}} {{ill|Ōda, Shimane|en|Ōda, Shimane|qid=Q384796|lt=Ota City|1=Ōda, Shimane}}, Kawai-cho) * Karamatsusan Tenjingu Shrine ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|qid=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}} {{ill|Daisen, Akita|en|Daisen, Akita|qid=Q695920|lt=Daisen City|1=Daisen, Akita}} Kyowa) * In September of the 7th year of the reign of Emperor Sujin, the deity Amaterasu Kuniteruhikohikamyo Kushitama Neihatsuhikonomikoto was given the posthumous title of Amaterasu Omikami. Later, Emperor Keiko gave him the posthumous title of Amaterasu. Masumida Shrine * [[Masumida Shrine]] - The first shrine of Owari Province. (The deity Amahimyo-no-mikoto is also called Amateru Kuniteruhiko Amahimyo-no-mikoto in the shrine tradition. * [[Kono Shrine|Kago Shrine]] - The first shrine in Moto-Ise. (The deity Hikohimyo-no-mikoto is also known as Amateru Kuniteru Hikohimyo-no-mikoto, or Hotohotemikoto, according to the shrine tradition. == See also == * [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]] == Notes == <references group="lower-alpha" responsive="1"></references> == References == <references />
[[en:Amenohoakari]]
<!--In addition, the {{ill|Iokibe clan|ja|伊福部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iokibe clan|qq=}} has a [[genealogy]] that incorporates Rokuhayoninomikoto into a genealogy that begins with Okikinomikoto, but this is said to be a genealogy that represents a son-in-law {{Citation needed|date=January 2021|qq=}}.-->
[[en:Amenohoakari]]<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[fr:Ame-no-hoakari]] [[ja:天火明命]] [[ko:아메노호아카리]] [[mni:ꯑꯃꯦꯅꯣꯍꯣꯑꯀꯔꯤ]] [[zh:天火明命]] {{wikidata link|Q10940411}} [[Category:Solar deities]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Agricultural deities]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
